rulings.Case Title: FEDERAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CHRONOMITE LABORATORIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 17, 2018, Line 1, CROSS-COMPLAINANT CHRONOMITE LABORATORIES, INC's Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint The Court grants Chronomite Laboratories, Inc.'s motion for leave to amend cross-complaint. No opposition was filed. The amended cross-complaint shall be filed no later than 20 days from the date of the order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Chronomite must prepare the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. -302/ac

rulings.Case Title: NIKKI HORNER ET AL VS. RARECULTURE LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 17, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT RARECULTURE LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The Court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend as to each cause of action. The court sustains the demurrer as to the First Cause of Action with leave to amend because Plaintiffs fail to allege facts specifically alleging that Defendant provided the allegedly unsafe scaffolding or exercised control over it. (McKown v. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. (2002) 27 Cal.4th 219, 225.) The court sustains the demurrer as to the Second Cause of Action with leave to amend because Plaintiffs fail to allege facts that Defendant retained control over the worksite in a way that affirmatively contributed to the injury. (Kinsman v. Unocal Corp. (2005) 37 Cal.App.4th 1439, 1446.) Passive failure to exercise retained control does not constitute an affirmative contribution to the injuries; and Plaintiffs do not allege facts that Defendant agreed to provide safety measures. (Tverberg v. Fillner Constr. Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1439, 1446.) The court sustains the demurrer as to the Third Cause of Action with leave to amend. Plaintiffs' allegations are supported by information in their opposition that is not present in the complaint. Plaintiffs allege that they are unlicensed painters and employees of Defendant and allege that Defendant is uninsured in violation of Labor Code section 3706. Under Blew v. Horner (1986) 187 Cal. App.3d 1380, 1382-83 and Labor Code Section 2750.5, unlicensed contractors who would normally require a license are employees as a matter of law. However, the allegation regarding Labor Code section 2750.5 and Plaintiffs being employees as a matter of law does not appear in the complaint. Plaintiffs should amend their complaint to more specifically plead allegations of the employer-employee relationship and how that triggers the alleged statutory violation for the lack of workers compensation insurance. The court sustains the demurrer as to the Fourth Cause of Action with leave to amend. Plaintiffs fail to allege any facts to support the claim that Defendant designed or manufactured the scaffolding or introduced it into the stream of commerce. (Cadlo v. Owens-Ill. Inc., (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 513, 516 [holding that a strict products liability claim requires an allegation that defendant had an actual connection with the design, manufacture, or distribution of the defective product.]) The court sustains the demurrer as to the Fifth Cause of Action with leave to amend. Plaintiffs allege that scaffolding was provided by NorCal Rental Group and supplied by Centric Construction, Inc., not by RAREculture. A defendant cannot be liable for defects in another manufacturer's product. (O'Neil v. Crane Co. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 335, 351.) As currently pleaded, Defendant cannot be found liable for negligent failure to warn. Plaintiff must file her amended complaint no later than 20 days from the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant Rareculture LLC must prepare the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/ac

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BERGQUIST VS. SHOTSKI LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 17, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT SHOTSKI, LLC D/B/A SILVER SPUR's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The Court overrules defendant Shotski, LLC, dba Silver Spur's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint. Plaintiff John Bergquist sufficiently alleges facts that he was falsely accused of a crime, thus supporting a claim under Civ. Code § 46. Penal Code § 311.2 is a crime punishable by imprisonment. (Penal Code § 311.9.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff John Bergquist must prepare the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ac

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BERGQUIST VS. SHOTSKI LLC ET AL
